title: At the Bank
description: Practice banking conversations for common financial services

lines.speaker: Bank Officer
lines.text: Good morning! How can I help you today?

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Good morning. I'd like to open a bank account. I recently moved to this city and I need a local account.

lines.speaker: Bank Officer
lines.text: Welcome! We'd be happy to help. We offer several account types. Are you looking for a personal checking account, a savings account, or both?

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: I'd like both, actually. I need a checking account for daily expenses and a savings account to build an emergency fund.

lines.speaker: Bank Officer
lines.text: Great choice. Our Smart Checking account has no monthly fee as long as you maintain a minimum balance of $500 or receive at least one direct deposit per month. It also includes a free debit card and mobile banking.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: That sounds good. What about the savings account?

lines.speaker: Bank Officer
lines.text: Our High-Yield Savings account currently earns 4.5% APY with no minimum balance requirement. You can link it to your checking account for easy transfers.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: 4.5% is impressive. Is that rate guaranteed?

lines.speaker: Bank Officer
lines.text: It's a variable rate, so it can change based on market conditions, but we've maintained competitive rates consistently. Rates are reviewed quarterly.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Understood. What do I need to open these accounts?

lines.speaker: Bank Officer
lines.text: You'll need two forms of identification — typically a passport or driver's license — plus proof of your current address, such as a utility bill or lease agreement.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: I have my passport and a copy of my lease agreement. Is that sufficient?

lines.speaker: Bank Officer
lines.text: Perfect. I'll also need your Social Security Number or ITIN, and an initial deposit. The minimum opening deposit for checking is $25 and for savings is $50.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: I can do $200 for checking and $500 for savings. I want to start building that emergency fund right away.

lines.speaker: Bank Officer
lines.text: Excellent start. Let me take your documents and we'll fill in the application form together.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: How long does the process take?

lines.speaker: Bank Officer
lines.text: About 20–30 minutes. Your accounts will be active immediately. You'll receive your debit card in the mail within 5–7 business days, but you can use your account number for online transactions right away.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Perfect. Can I also set up online banking and the mobile app today?

lines.speaker: Bank Officer
lines.text: Absolutely. I'll walk you through the mobile app setup before you leave. It allows you to deposit checks by taking a photo, transfer money, and set up alerts for all transactions.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: That's very convenient. One more question — what's the overdraft policy?

lines.speaker: Bank Officer
lines.text: We offer overdraft protection that links your savings account to your checking account. If your checking balance drops below zero, we automatically transfer funds from savings at no fee. Without this link, there's a $35 overdraft fee per transaction.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: I'd like to set up the overdraft protection, please.

lines.speaker: Bank Officer
lines.text: Of course. I'll include that in your setup. Let's start with your passport, please.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Here you go. I'm also wondering — is there a way to increase the savings interest rate further?

lines.speaker: Bank Officer
lines.text: Yes. If you maintain a balance over $10,000 in savings, the rate bumps up to 4.8%. And our 12-month CD currently offers 5.2% if you want to lock in a portion of your funds.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: I'll keep that in mind for the future. For now, let's proceed with the two accounts.

lines.speaker: Bank Officer
lines.text: Wonderful. Welcome to First National Bank. We're glad to have you as a customer.

lines.speaker: Loan Officer
lines.text: Good afternoon. I'm James, a Loan Officer. I understand you're interested in a personal loan?

lines.speaker: Applicant
lines.text: Yes, that's right. I need to fund some home renovations — mainly a kitchen remodel and bathroom upgrade. I'm looking at around $30,000.

lines.speaker: Loan Officer
lines.text: A personal loan is a great option for home improvements. Could you share a bit about your financial situation so we can find the best fit?

lines.speaker: Applicant
lines.text: Sure. I've been employed for six years at the same company with a stable income of about $85,000 annually. My credit score was 740 the last time I checked.

lines.speaker: Loan Officer
lines.text: A 740 credit score puts you in the 'good' category — you'll likely qualify for our most competitive rates. Currently, our personal loan rates for qualified borrowers start at 7.9% APR.

lines.speaker: Applicant
lines.text: What would my monthly payments look like on $30,000 at that rate?

lines.speaker: Loan Officer
lines.text: For a 5-year term at 7.9%, your monthly payment would be approximately $608. Total interest paid over the life of the loan would be around $6,480.

lines.speaker: Applicant
lines.text: Is there flexibility on the term? I'd prefer a lower monthly payment if possible.

lines.speaker: Loan Officer
lines.text: We offer terms of 3, 5, and 7 years. At 7 years, your monthly payment drops to about $466, but total interest increases to approximately $9,144.

lines.speaker: Applicant
lines.text: I see the trade-off. What if I want to pay it off early? Are there prepayment penalties?

lines.speaker: Loan Officer
lines.text: No prepayment penalties whatsoever. Many customers start with a 7-year term for flexibility and pay extra each month to reduce total interest.

lines.speaker: Applicant
lines.text: That's a smart strategy. What's the application process?

lines.speaker: Loan Officer
lines.text: It's straightforward. I'll need proof of income — recent pay stubs or tax returns — bank statements from the past three months, your ID, and we'll run a credit check with your permission.

lines.speaker: Applicant
lines.text: How long does approval take?

lines.speaker: Loan Officer
lines.text: If all documents are in order, we can often give a decision within 24 hours. Funds are typically disbursed within 2–3 business days of approval.

lines.speaker: Applicant
lines.text: That's faster than I expected. One concern — I'm thinking of also refinancing my mortgage in the next year. Would taking this personal loan affect that?

lines.speaker: Loan Officer
lines.text: Good thinking to plan ahead. A new loan will temporarily dip your credit score by a few points and increase your debt-to-income ratio. I'd suggest confirming with a mortgage lender what DTI threshold they require before committing.

lines.speaker: Applicant
lines.text: That's very helpful advice. I think I'd like to proceed with the application for the 7-year term and pay extra when I can.

lines.speaker: Loan Officer
lines.text: Great. Let me pull up the application. I'll need about 15 minutes of your time now, and I'll send you a secure link to upload your documents digitally when you get home.

lines.speaker: Applicant
lines.text: That's very convenient. Let's proceed.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Hi, I need to send money internationally to my family in the Philippines. Can you help me with that?

lines.speaker: Teller
lines.text: Of course. We can process an international wire transfer for you. How much would you like to send?

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: I'd like to send $2,000 US dollars. How will it be received — in dollars or in Philippine pesos?

lines.speaker: Teller
lines.text: The recipient will receive it in Philippine pesos at the current exchange rate. Today's rate is approximately 55.8 pesos per dollar, so they'd receive roughly 111,600 pesos.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: What fees are involved?

lines.speaker: Teller
lines.text: Our outgoing wire transfer fee is $35 for international transfers. The receiving bank may also charge a small fee on their end, usually around $10 to $20 equivalent.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: How long does it take for the money to arrive?

lines.speaker: Teller
lines.text: International wire transfers typically take one to three business days. Transfers to the Philippines usually arrive within two business days.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: That's reasonable. What information do I need to provide?

lines.speaker: Teller
lines.text: I'll need the recipient's full name as it appears on their bank account, their bank name, branch address, account number, and the bank's SWIFT code.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: I have all of that written down. Here's the information.

lines.speaker: Teller
lines.text: Let me enter the details. I'll also need your government-issued ID for compliance purposes, since this is an international transfer.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Here's my driver's license. Is there a limit on how much I can send?

lines.speaker: Teller
lines.text: There's no limit on wire transfers, but amounts over $10,000 require additional documentation under federal regulations. Your $2,000 transfer is straightforward.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Will I get a confirmation when the money arrives?

lines.speaker: Teller
lines.text: You'll receive an email confirmation when the transfer is processed on our end. For confirmation of receipt, you'd need to check with the recipient or their bank.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Perfect. Please go ahead and process it.

lines.speaker: Agent
lines.text: Thank you for calling First National Bank. How can I assist you?

lines.speaker: Cardholder
lines.text: I need to report my credit card as lost. I think I left it at a restaurant last night and when I called them this morning, they said they don't have it.

lines.speaker: Agent
lines.text: I'm sorry to hear that. Let me pull up your account right away. Can you verify your name, date of birth, and the last four digits of the card?

lines.speaker: Cardholder
lines.text: David Park, October 3rd, 1988. The last four digits are 7241.

lines.speaker: Agent
lines.text: Thank you, Mr. Park. I've located your account. Let me freeze the card immediately so no one can use it. Have you noticed any unauthorized charges?

lines.speaker: Cardholder
lines.text: I checked my app this morning and there's a charge for $87 at a gas station that I didn't make. I haven't been to a gas station in weeks.

lines.speaker: Agent
lines.text: I can see that charge. I'll flag it as fraudulent right away. You will not be held responsible for any unauthorized transactions. I'll initiate a dispute and a provisional credit of $87 will appear on your statement within 48 hours.

lines.speaker: Cardholder
lines.text: That's a relief. How do I get a replacement card?

lines.speaker: Agent
lines.text: I'll issue a new card with a different number immediately. Standard delivery takes five to seven business days. We also offer expedited delivery within two business days for a $25 fee. Which would you prefer?

lines.speaker: Cardholder
lines.text: I'll take the expedited delivery. I use the card for a lot of online subscriptions and automatic payments.

lines.speaker: Agent
lines.text: Good point. Once you receive the new card, you'll need to update your card number on any recurring payments — things like streaming services, gym memberships, and utility bills.

lines.speaker: Cardholder
lines.text: Is there a way to see which merchants have my card on file?

lines.speaker: Agent
lines.text: Yes, in our mobile app under 'Recurring Payments,' you can see a list of merchants that regularly charge your card. That should help you update everything.

lines.speaker: Cardholder
lines.text: That's very useful. Thank you for handling this so quickly.

lines.speaker: Agent
lines.text: You're welcome. Your new card ending in 5893 will arrive within two business days. Is there anything else I can help with?

lines.speaker: Homebuyer
lines.text: Good morning. My spouse and I are looking to buy our first home and we'd like to understand our mortgage options.

lines.speaker: Mortgage Advisor
lines.text: Congratulations! Buying your first home is exciting. I'd be happy to walk you through the options. Do you have a property in mind, or are you still looking?

lines.speaker: Homebuyer
lines.text: We're still looking, but we want to get pre-approved first so we know our budget. We've been saving for a down payment for three years.

lines.speaker: Mortgage Advisor
lines.text: Getting pre-approved is a smart move. It shows sellers you're serious and helps you focus your search. What's your combined annual income?

lines.speaker: Homebuyer
lines.text: Together we make about $130,000. We have $60,000 saved for a down payment.

lines.speaker: Mortgage Advisor
lines.text: That's a solid position. With that income and down payment, you'd likely qualify for a home in the $350,000 to $400,000 range, depending on your other debts.

lines.speaker: Homebuyer
lines.text: We have some student loans — about $25,000 remaining — and one car payment.

lines.speaker: Mortgage Advisor
lines.text: I'll factor those into the debt-to-income calculation. Now, let me explain the main mortgage types. A 30-year fixed rate gives you predictable monthly payments — our current rate is 6.5%. A 15-year fixed rate is 5.8%, which means higher monthly payments but you pay much less interest overall.

lines.speaker: Homebuyer
lines.text: What about adjustable rate mortgages? A friend mentioned those might have lower initial rates.

lines.speaker: Mortgage Advisor
lines.text: Good question. Our 5/1 ARM starts at 5.5% for the first five years, then adjusts annually based on market rates. It's lower initially but carries the risk of higher payments later. For first-time buyers planning to stay long-term, I generally recommend fixed rates for stability.

lines.speaker: Homebuyer
lines.text: We plan to stay at least ten years, so fixed makes more sense for us. How much would we pay monthly on a $340,000 home with a $60,000 down payment?

lines.speaker: Mortgage Advisor
lines.text: On a $280,000 mortgage at 6.5% for 30 years, your principal and interest would be about $1,770 per month. Add property taxes and insurance, and you're looking at roughly $2,200 to $2,400 monthly depending on the area.

lines.speaker: Homebuyer
lines.text: That's within what we'd be comfortable paying. What's the next step?

lines.speaker: Mortgage Advisor
lines.text: I'll need your last two years of tax returns, recent pay stubs, bank statements showing your savings, and permission to run a credit check. We can often complete pre-approval within three business days.

lines.speaker: Homebuyer
lines.text: We'll gather those documents and come back this week. Thank you for explaining everything so clearly.

lines.speaker: Mortgage Advisor
lines.text: My pleasure. Buying a home is a big decision, and I'm here to guide you through every step.
